rationale: De Angelis et al. (2012) is used in theory development rather than only in a generic literature list: the citing paper links it to a specific claim that self-enhancement motivates sharing positive information about one's own experiences, which supports the self-presentation logic behind its hypotheses. The citation also appears in methodological framing (text-based WOM outcomes) and again in the General Discussion to interpret results about acquisitive versus protective self-presentation. While the citing paper proposes a different main mechanism (audience size and sharer focus), De Angelis et al. provides meaningful conceptual and empirical grounding.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on De Angelis et al. (2012) as prior evidence that self-enhancement shapes WOM valence, using it to motivate and interpret its self-presentation predictions about what people share. It also cites that work as precedent for using linguistic message content as a WOM-relevant outcome.
